skill.name: subagent-driven-development
skill.description: Execute implementation plans by dispatching fresh subagents per task with two-stage review
skill.summary: A workflow skill that executes plans using fresh subagents for each task with spec compliance and code quality reviews
skill.author: ZhanlinCui

security_audit.summary: Static analyzer flagged 38 patterns but all are false positives. DOT diagram syntax (backticks), markdown formatting (crypto patterns), and self-review checklists (reconnaissance patterns) were incorrectly identified. No actual security risks exist - this is a legitimate workflow orchestration skill.

risk_factor_evidence.reasoning: DOT diagram syntax (digraph, rankdir) in markdown code blocks, not shell execution
Path shown in documentation example only (line 107 shows ~/.config as example location), not actual file operations

content.user_title: Execute Plans with Subagents and Two-Stage Review
content.value_statement: AI agents often struggle with complex multi-task implementations. This skill solves that by dispatching fresh subagents for each task with mandatory spec compliance and code quality reviews.
actual_capabilities: Dispatches fresh subagents for each implementation task to avoid context pollution
Enforces two-stage review: spec compliance first, then code quality
Maintains continuous progress within same session without handoffs
Supports self-review before handoff and review loops until approval
Integrates with TDD practices and plan execution workflows
limitations: Requires a pre-written implementation plan with clearly defined tasks
Not suitable for tightly coupled tasks that require parallel coordination
Higher token usage due to multiple subagent invocations per task
Requires human to answer clarifying questions before implementation proceeds

use_cases.title: Feature implementation from specification
use_cases.description: Execute a multi-feature implementation plan where each feature can be developed independently
use_cases.target_user: Tech lead managing complex feature rollouts

use_cases.title: Refactoring large codebases
use_cases.description: Break down refactoring into discrete tasks with quality gates to ensure correctness

use_cases.title: Test-driven development at scale
use_cases.description: Implement multiple components with TDD, ensuring each passes tests before moving forward

prompt_templates.title: Basic: Single Task Implementation
prompt_templates.prompt: I'm using Subagent-Driven Development. Task 1: Create a function to validate email addresses. Context: This will be used in the user registration flow. Please implement with tests.
prompt_templates.scenario: Implementing a single well-defined utility function

prompt_templates.title: Intermediate: Multi-Task Feature
prompt_templates.prompt: I'm using Subagent-Driven Development to implement user authentication. Task 1: Create login endpoint with password validation. Task 2: Add session management. Task 3: Implement logout with token invalidation. Each task includes full text from my plan document.
prompt_templates.scenario: Building a complete feature with multiple independent components

prompt_templates.title: Advanced: Full Plan Execution
prompt_templates.prompt: I'm executing a plan using Subagent-Driven Development. I've created a TodoWrite with 8 tasks extracted from docs/plans/api-v2-migration.md. Starting Task 1: Migrate user endpoints to v2 format. Here is the full task text and context about the migration strategy.
prompt_templates.scenario: Executing a comprehensive migration or refactoring plan

prompt_templates.title: Expert: With Custom Review Criteria
prompt_templates.prompt: Using Subagent-Driven Development for Task 3: Implement rate limiting. Custom review criteria: Must handle distributed environments, support configurable limits per endpoint, include metrics collection. Spec reviewer should verify these criteria. Code reviewer should check Redis integration patterns.
prompt_templates.scenario: Adding custom quality gates for specialized requirements

output_examples.input: Task: Add input validation to form component
output_examples.output: Implementer reports: Added validation for email, phone, and required fields. 12/12 tests passing. Self-review: Added error messages matching design system. Spec reviewer: Approved - all validation rules implemented. Code reviewer: Approved - clean implementation with proper error handling.

Implementer reports: Created migration adding users table with indexes. Rollback script included. Self-review: Followed existing migration patterns. Spec reviewer: Approved. Code reviewer: Approved - migration is idempotent and well-documented.

best_practices: Always provide full task text and context upfront - do not make subagents read plan files
Answer all clarifying questions before allowing implementation to begin
Enforce the review order: spec compliance must pass before code quality review starts
anti_patterns: Skipping either review stage or proceeding with unfixed issues
Dispatching multiple implementation subagents in parallel which can cause conflicts
Accepting self-review as a substitute for independent review

faq.question: When should I use this versus parallel session execution?
faq.answer: Use this skill when you want to stay in the same session with continuous progress. Use parallel session execution when tasks can run completely independently without any coordination.

faq.question: What happens if the spec reviewer finds issues?
faq.answer: The implementer subagent fixes the issues, then the spec reviewer reviews again. This loop continues until the spec is fully compliant before code quality review begins.

faq.question: Can subagents ask questions during implementation?
faq.answer: Yes. Subagents are encouraged to ask questions at any point if they encounter something unclear. Answering questions before proceeding prevents incorrect implementations.

faq.question: How many reviewers are involved per task?
faq.answer: Three: the implementer does self-review, then a spec compliance reviewer checks requirements, then a code quality reviewer checks implementation quality.

faq.question: What if a subagent fails to complete a task?
faq.answer: Dispatch a fix subagent with specific instructions about what needs to be corrected. Do not try to fix manually as this causes context pollution.

faq.question: Is this skill compatible with all AI assistants?
faq.answer: Yes, it works with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code. The subagent dispatch mechanism uses the Task tool available in these platforms.
